description: Immutable feature list for FORGE. Agents MUST NOT delete or modify feature descriptions or success criteria. Mark passes: true when a feature is complete with all criteria met.

phase_1.description: Core task execution loop. Implemented by Factory.ai Mission.

features.name: view_file tool
features.description: Stateful file viewer that shows num_lines lines starting from start_line, with line numbers. Remembers position across calls within a session. Returns structured ViewResult JSON.
success_criteria: Returns JSON with path, start_line, end_line, total_lines, content (with line numbers), truncated flag
Bounds checking: cannot read beyond file length, max 200 lines per call
Position memory: calling view_file(path) without start_line continues from last position
Handles binary files gracefully (returns error, does not crash)
Test: test_view_file_basic, test_view_file_bounds, test_view_file_position_memory, test_view_file_binary

features.name: edit_file tool
features.description: Replace lines start_line:end_line with new_content. Runs lint check BEFORE applying. Rejects edits that introduce syntax errors. Returns structured EditResult JSON.
success_criteria: Returns JSON with path, start_line, end_line, lines_added, lines_removed, lint_status, applied flag
Lint check runs before applying edit — if lint fails, edit is NOT applied and error is returned with line/column
Supports Python (.py) and TypeScript (.ts) lint checking at minimum
Creates backup before applying (for rollback)
Test: test_edit_file_basic, test_edit_file_lint_reject, test_edit_file_rollback

features.name: search_file tool
features.description: Search for regex or literal pattern in a file. Returns max max_results matches with surrounding context. Returns structured SearchResult JSON.
success_criteria: Returns JSON with path, pattern, matches (array of {line_number, content, context_before, context_after}), total_matches, truncated flag
Max 50 results by default, configurable
Context: 2 lines before and after each match
Supports regex and literal modes
Test: test_search_file_literal, test_search_file_regex, test_search_file_bounds

features.name: run_command tool
features.description: Execute a shell command with timeout and allowlist checking. Returns structured CommandResult JSON.
success_criteria: Returns JSON with command, exit_code, stdout (truncated to 5000 chars), stderr (truncated to 2000 chars), duration_ms, timed_out flag
Allowlist enforcement: blocks commands matching dangerous patterns (rm -rf, curl|bash, sudo, etc.)
Timeout enforcement: kills process after timeout seconds
Test: test_run_command_basic, test_run_command_blocked, test_run_command_timeout

features.name: run_tests tool
features.description: Execute test suite (pytest) and return structured results. Returns structured TestResult JSON.
success_criteria: Returns JSON with test_path, passed, failed, errors, skipped, duration_ms, failures (array of {test_name, message, file, line})
Timeout enforcement
Handles missing test directory gracefully
Test: test_run_tests_pass, test_run_tests_fail, test_run_tests_timeout

features.name: codemap tool (tree-sitter)
features.description: Generate structural summary of a file using tree-sitter. Returns function/class/type signatures without implementation bodies. 10x token savings vs full file content.
success_criteria: Returns JSON with path, language, symbols (array of {name, kind, signature, start_line, end_line})
Supports Python and TypeScript at minimum
Extracts: function signatures, class definitions, type aliases, imports
Does NOT include function bodies — signatures only
Test: test_codemap_python, test_codemap_typescript, test_codemap_unsupported_language

features.name: Worker agent loop
features.description: Agent that receives a task description, uses ACI tools to implement it, and produces a git commit. Uses the configured local model via OpenAI-compatible API.
success_criteria: Accepts task description as input
Calls ACI tools via structured function calling (OpenAI tool_call format)
Produces at least one git commit with descriptive message in FORGE format
Handles tool errors gracefully (retries or reports)
Bounded: stops after max_iterations (configurable, default 20) or max_tokens (configurable)
Records all tool calls and model responses in trace
Test: test_worker_simple_task (with mock model and mock filesystem)

features.name: Oracle generator
features.description: Generate a Core Oracle snapshot after task completion. Produces structured JSON matching the Oracle schema from Architecture §4.
success_criteria: Generates diff summary (files_changed, insertions, deletions, patch)
Generates codemap of changed files via tree-sitter
Runs mechanical checks (lint, tests at minimum)
Includes quality delta (desloppify mechanical score before vs after)
Prompts worker model for self-assessment
Packages all into CoreOracle JSON matching schema
Token estimate: Core Oracle is 2-4K tokens for typical tasks
Test: test_oracle_generation (with a real diff fixture)

features.name: Gate engine (Phase 1: single reviewer)
features.description: Send Oracle to a single frontier reviewer. Process structured verdict. PASS → merge. FAIL → return TODO to worker.
success_criteria: Sends Core Oracle to configured frontier reviewer model
Reviewer receives structured prompt with Oracle + review instructions
Parses reviewer response into Verdict dataclass (status, issues, todos)
On PASS: returns approval signal
On FAIL: extracts structured TODOs (file, line, issue, fix_description, priority)
On AMBIGUOUS: escalates to second reviewer (if configured)
Handles API errors gracefully (retry with backoff)
Test: test_gate_pass, test_gate_fail, test_gate_api_error (with mock API)

features.name: Task iteration loop
features.description: Complete loop: worker → oracle → gate → if FAIL iterate → if PASS merge. With recovery mode after N failures.
success_criteria: Orchestrates: worker executes → oracle generated → gate reviews → verdict
On PASS: squash-merge to main with structured commit message
On FAIL: feeds TODO back to worker, re-executes, re-oracles, re-reviews
Recovery mode: after 3 consecutive failures, generates failure summary and stops (does not thrash)
Shadow mode: in Phase 1, proposes merge but waits for human confirmation
Records iteration count, tokens consumed (local + frontier), wall-clock time
Test: test_loop_pass_first_try, test_loop_fail_then_pass, test_loop_recovery_mode (with mocks)

features.name: Trace system
features.description: Record all model calls, tool calls, and decisions in DuckDB. Support queries for metrics and boundary measurement.
success_criteria: DuckDB database created at .forge/traces/forge.duckdb
Records: model calls (model, role, tokens_in, tokens_out, latency_ms, outcome)
Records: tool calls (tool_name, args, result_summary, duration_ms)
Records: task outcomes (task_id, iterations, pass_fail, error_taxonomy tags)
Queryable via SQL
Test: test_trace_record, test_trace_query

features.name: Error taxonomy tagging
features.description: Tag every task failure with one or more error taxonomy categories. Categories: tool_misuse, navigation_failure, incorrect_logic, missing_tests, architectural_drift, context_confusion, flaky_validation.
success_criteria: Frontier reviewer includes error_taxonomy tags in FAIL verdicts
Tags stored in trace database alongside task outcome
forge metrics shows error taxonomy distribution
Test: test_error_taxonomy_tagging (with mock verdict containing tags)

features.name: Boundary measurement
features.description: Track frontier/local boundary data. Record which tasks passed locally first-try vs needed frontier correction.
success_criteria: BoundaryRecord created for every completed task: task_type, local_model, first_pass_success, iterations_to_pass, frontier_tokens_consumed, error_taxonomy_tags, correction_description
forge boundary command outputs summary: first-pass rate, iteration distribution, error taxonomy breakdown, frontier cost per task
Data stored in DuckDB for querying
Test: test_boundary_record, test_boundary_summary

features.name: Benchmark cartridge runner
features.description: Run a fixed set of benchmark tasks against the current harness configuration. Produce comparable results.
success_criteria: Reads benchmark tasks from .forge/benchmarks/*.yaml
Executes each task through the full loop (worker → oracle → gate → verdict)
Records results per task: pass/fail, iterations, tokens, time, error taxonomy
forge benchmark command runs the suite and outputs results table
Results stored in .forge/benchmark-results/ for comparison across runs
Test: test_benchmark_runner (with 2-3 simple fixture tasks)

features.name: Desloppify mechanical scan
features.description: Tree-sitter based mechanical quality scan. Detects dead code, duplication, complexity. Zero GPU cost.
success_criteria: Scans Python files using tree-sitter
Detects: unused imports, unused variables, duplicate function signatures, high cyclomatic complexity (>10)
Produces score (0-100) and issue list
forge quality command runs scan and displays results
Score delta tracked between runs (stored in .forge/quality/)
Test: test_mechanical_scan (with fixture files containing known issues)

features.name: forge task CLI command (end-to-end)
features.description: The complete forge task command that ties everything together.
success_criteria: forge task 'description' runs the full loop from CLI
Configurable model assignments via .forge/config.yaml
Outputs progress to terminal (task started, oracle generated, review result, iteration count)
On completion: outputs summary (pass/fail, iterations, tokens, frontier cost)
Shadow mode: prints proposed commit and waits for user confirmation before merging
Trace data recorded for every run
Test: test_forge_task_e2e (integration test with mock models)

features.name: forge status and forge metrics commands
features.description: Status shows current state. Metrics shows accumulated measurement data.
success_criteria: forge status: current task state, feature_list progress, recent activity
forge metrics: total tasks run, first-pass rate, iteration distribution, error taxonomy breakdown, frontier/local token split, cost summary
Both read from DuckDB trace database
Test: test_status_command, test_metrics_command

phase_2_plus.description: Advanced features. Implemented by Claw builder with FORGE self-review. NOT in scope for Factory.ai.
